test_text.title: Booking a Whale Watch Holiday with Dreamtime Travel

audioscript.text: Section 1, you will hear a telephone conversation between a travel consultant and a customer.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example which has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good afternoon, Dreamtime Travel.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Oh, hello. I'm interested in the holidays you offer along the coast near here.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Yes, we operate several tours up the coast.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Where in particular did you want to go?
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Well, I like the sound of the holiday that mentioned whales.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Was it whale watching?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Oh, that's our whale watch experience, it's very popular.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: And it's based in a lovely little town with nice beaches.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: The holiday is called Whale Watch experience.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So Whale Watch experience has been written on the form.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully, and answer questions 1 to 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good afternoon, Dreamtime Travel.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Oh, hello. I'm interested in the holidays you offer along the coast near here.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Yes, we operate several tours up the coast.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Where in particular did you want to go?
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Well, I like the sound of the holiday that mentioned whales.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Was it whale watching?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Oh, that's our whale watch experience.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: It's very popular.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: And it's based in a lovely little town with nice beaches.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Oh, right.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: And how long does it last?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: It's 2 days, that includes 4 hours travel time each way from here.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Good, I don't want to be away any longer than that.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: So is that by coach?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Actually it's by minibus.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: We like to keep those tours small and personal, so we don't take a whole coach load of people. In fact, we only take up to 15 people on this tour, although we do run it with just 12 or 13.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Oh, right.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: So do you run these tours often?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Well, it depends on the time of year.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Of course, in peak times like the summer holidays, we do them every weekend, but at the moment, it's usually once a month at most.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: And when is the next one going?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Hmm. Let me see.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Uh. There's one in three weeks' time, which is April the 18th, and we don't have another one until June the second.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: All right.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Um. And is April a good time to go?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Pretty good, though the really good time is later in the year.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: I have to say though that the whale sighting is only one of the many things offered.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Really?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Yes, the hotel itself where you stay has great facilities.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: It's called the Pallisades.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Uh. The Paris what?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: No, it's actually the Pallisades.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: PALLISADES.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: It's right on the main beach there.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Oh, I see.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: All of the rooms have nice views, and the food is really good there too.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Oh, right.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 5 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 5 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: And what about the other things?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Um. You know that are included in the price?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Oh, there are lots of things.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: But if you don't want to do the whale watch cruise, your guide will take anyone who is interested either on a bush walk.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Through the national park near the hotel, and there's no extra charge for that, or on a fishing trip.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: That's an extra $12 I think.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: And there's also a reptile park in town, that costs more or less the same.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: No, I think I'd prefer whales to snakes.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Yeah, and if you just want to relax, you're free to sit by the hotel pool or go down the beach.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Oh, and they also have tennis courts at the hotel, but you have to pay for those by the hour.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: But there are table tennis tables downstairs, and they are part of the accommodation package, just speak to your guide.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Well, that sounds good.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Um. So how much is the basic tour price?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: At this time of year, it's usually around $300 but let me check.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Um. Oh, it's actually $280.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: And the next tour, are there any places on that one?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: How many people is it for?
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: There are two of us.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Yes, that should be fine.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Uh. Can I just mention that we require all bookings to be made at least 14 days before you travel to avoid cancellations of tours?
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: And if you cancel within 7 days of departure, you will have to pay 50% of your total booking.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: And you also need to pay a 20% deposit,
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: at the time of booking.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Can I pay that by credit card?
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Yes, you can.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: All right, uh, what I'll do is I'll talk to my partner and get back to you.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: So I'll make a provisional booking, shall I?
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: 2 for the whale Watch experience.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Uh. Let me issue you with a customer reference number for when you call back.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Do you have a pen?
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Yes.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: OK, it's 3 9 7 4 5 T, that's T for tango.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: When you call back, ask to speak to the tour manager.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: That's me, Tracy.
audioscript.speaker: RECEPTIONIST

audioscript.text: Fine, I will.
audioscript.speaker: CUSTOMER

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

desc.constraint: NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER

body.title: Which TWO things are included in the price of the tour?

desc.constraint: NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER

items: Bookings must be made no later than <input> days in advance.

answer_key.correct_answer: 20% | 20 per cent | 20 percent | twenty per cent | twenty percent

test_text.title: A Review of Baby Cots and Tips for Choosing Them

audioscript.text: Section 2, you will hear a radio program giving parents advice about buying cots for their babies to sleep in.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 17.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 17.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Hello, and welcome to today's Buyer Beware program, where we give you some tips on how to spend your money wisely.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, in today's show, we're looking at beds for children and babies.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Let's start by looking at baby cots.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That's for children of up to 3 years old.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We tested three different cots, all in the budget price range, and as usual, we will feature the good points, the problems, and our verdict.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The first cot we looked at was by Baby Safe, and it had several good points to recommend it.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Our testers liked the fact that it had four wheels, so it was easy to move around.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The only slight problems with this cot were that it had no brakes, but they didn't think that mattered too much.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: At first, they were a bit concerned about the side bar, because they felt babies could trap their fingers in it.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: But our testers felt that this was unlikely to happen, so they've given this one a verdict of satisfactory.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The next cot was by Choice Cots, and this time our testers were pleased to find a cot which is simple to put together, unlike others we looked at.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: On the minus side, our testers did not like the fact that the side of the cot did not drop down, making it difficult to pick up Newborn babies.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: However, the real problem with this cot was the space between the bars; our testers found they were too wide, and a baby could easily trap his head.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We felt this was a real safety hazard, and so we've labeled this one dangerous.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: I'm afraid.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the program, you have some time to look at questions 18 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 18 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: And finally, better news for the Mother's Choice cot.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This cot was slightly different in that although the side bar did not drop down, the base could be raised or lowered into two different positions, making it safe as well as convenient.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The negatives for this one were quite minor.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The only niggle everyone had was the fact that it has no wheels, and the only other problem anyone could find was that there were pictures.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Which were simply stuck on and so could easily become detached.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The makers have now promised to discontinue this practice; as this cot will then be safe in every way, we have made the Mother's Choice cot our best buy.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Congratulations, Mother's Choice!
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So what features should you look for in a baby's cot?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Well, obviously safety is a very important factor, as well as comfort and convenience.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We recommend that if you are buying a cot.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Do make sure that any metal present is not rusted or bent in any way.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: You should ensure your cot has only rounded or smooth edging without any sharp edges.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This is especially important for wooden cots, and now onto beds for toddlers.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: But our testers felt that this was unlikely to happen, so they've given this one a verdict of satisfactory.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The next cot was by Choice Cots, and this time our testers were pleased to find a cot which is simple to put together, unlike others we looked at.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: On the minus side, our testers did not like the fact that the side of the cot did not drop down, making it difficult to pick up Newborn babies.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: However, the real problem with this cot was the space between the bars; our testers found they were too wide, and a baby could easily trap his head.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We felt this was a real safety hazard, and so we've labeled this one dangerous.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: I'm afraid.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Before you hear the rest of the program, you have some time to look at questions 18 to 20.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Now listen and answer questions 18 to 20.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: And finally, better news for the Mother's Choice cot.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: This cot was slightly different in that although the side bar did not drop down, the base could be raised or lowered into two different positions, making it safe as well as convenient.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The negatives for this one were quite minor.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The only niggle everyone had was the fact that it has no wheels, and the only other problem anyone could find was that there were pictures.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Which were simply stuck on and so could easily become detached.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The makers have now promised to discontinue this practice; as this cot will then be safe in every way, we have made the Mother's Choice cot our best buy.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Congratulations, Mother's Choice!
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: So what features should you look for in a baby's cot?
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Well, obviously safety is a very important factor, as well as comfort and convenience.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We recommend that if you are buying a cot.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Do make sure that any metal present is not rusted or bent in any way.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: You should ensure your cot has only rounded or smooth edging without any sharp edges.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: This is especially important for wooden cots, and now onto beds for toddlers.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That is the end of section 2.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items: Babies could trap their <input> in the side bar
Base of cot could be moved
Metal should not be rusted or bent
Edges of cot should not be <input>

answer_key.correct_answer: best | the best | best buy | the best buy | safe

test_text.title: Andrew's Discussion with Monica about Management Diploma Courses

audioscript.text: Section 3, you will hear a conversation between a student Andrew and a student advisor Monica about a diploma course.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Excuse me, I was told to come here for advice about management diploma courses.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: You've certainly come to the right place.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Hi, my name is Monica.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Hmm. Nice to meet you.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: My name is Andrew, Andrew Harris.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: So, Andrew, have you seen our diploma course prospectus yet?
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Yes, I've already looked at it.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: In fact, I thought the information on course content was really useful.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: But I'm afraid I'm a bit confused by all the different ways you can do the course: full-time intensive, part-time, and so on.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: Well, let's see if I can help.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: I think each course type has its advantages and disadvantages, so it really depends on you, your own study habits, and your financial circumstances of course.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Are you working at the moment?
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Yes, I've been working in the administration section of the local hospital for the last 3 years.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: And before that, I worked in the office of a computer engineering company for two years.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: So I've got about 5 years of relevant work experience, and what I'm hoping to focus on is personnel management.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: And are you planning to leave your current job to study, or are you thinking about just taking a year off?
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: I want to know what my options are really.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: I don't want to quit my job or anything, and my employers are keen for me to get some more qualifications, but obviously, it would be better if I could do a course without taking too much time away from work.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: So you don't really want to do the full-time course then?
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: No, not really.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: It's also a question of finances, you see, my office have agreed to pay the cost of the course itself, but I would have to take unpaid leave if I want to study full-time, and, well, I don't think I could afford to support myself with no salary for a whole year.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: Hmm, OK.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Well, you have two other possibilities.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: You could either do the part-time course.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: That would be over two years, and you wouldn't have to take any time off work, or you could do what we call a modular course.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: You could do that in 18 months if you wanted, or longer.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: It's quite flexible, and it would be up to you.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Hmm, uh, so what does the part-time course involve?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: For that, you would join an evening class and have a lecture twice a week.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Then you'd have to attend a seminar or discussion workshop one weekend a month.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: W- what kind of coursework would I have to do?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: Well it's a mixture.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: You'd be expected to write an essay each month, which counts towards your final assessment.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: You have a case study to do by the end of the course, which might involve doing a survey or something like that, and also you need to hand in a short report every four weeks.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: So that's quite a lot of work then, on top of working every day.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: It sounds like a lot of studying, and really tiring.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: Yep, you certainly wouldn't have much free time.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Uh. What about the modular course?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: What would I have to do for that?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: Well, that's where you get the opportunity to study full-time for short periods.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: That way you can cover a lot of coursework, and attend lectures and seminars during the day.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: And each module lasts for one term, say about 12 weeks at a time.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: There are obvious advantages in this, the main one being that you can study in a much more intensive way, which suits some people much better.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: And how many of these 'modules' would I have to do to get the diploma?
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: The current program is two modules - and then you have to choose a topic to work in more depth - but you can base that on your job, and so you don't need to be away from the office, and how long it takes is up to you.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: The important thing is that you don't have to study and work. You can focus on one thing at a time.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Yes, I can see that.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: Uh. It certainly sounds attractive, it would be more expensive though.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: I mean I'd have to support myself without pay for each module.
audioscript.speaker: ANDREW

audioscript.text: Hmm. That's true, so that might be a problem for you.
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: Look, why don't you talk this over with your employers and...
audioscript.speaker: MONICA

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: Gender Differences in Personal Finance Saving Attitudes and Consequences

audioscript.text: Section 4, you will hear part of a lecture given by an economist about North American women's attitude to money and saving.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully, and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: OK, so we've been looking at the attitudes of various social and cultural groups towards the management of their personal finances, how important they feel it is to save money.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And what they save their money for.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: One aspect that we haven't yet considered is gender.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So if we consider gender issues, we're basically asking whether men and women have different attitudes toward saving money.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And whether they save money for different things.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Back in 1928, the British writer George Bernard Shaw wrote in his Intelligent Women's Guide to Socialism and Capitalism, that a man is supposed to understand politics, economics and finance.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And is therefore unwilling to accept essential instruction.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: He also said, a woman having fewer pretensions is far more willing to learn.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, though these days, people might question a lot of the assumptions contained in those statements, recent research does suggest that there are some quite fundamental differences between men and women.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In their attitudes to economic matters.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Let's look at what men and women actually save for.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Research studies of women in North America have found that women are far more likely to save for their children's education, and they are also more likely to save up in order to buy a house one day.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The same studies have found that men on the other hand tend to save for a car, which by the way takes a surprisingly large amount of the household budget in North America.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: But the other main priority for men when saving money is their retirement.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: When they're earning, they're far more likely to put money aside for their old age than women are.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, this is rather disturbing, because in fact the need for women to save for their old age is far greater than for men.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Let's consider this for a moment.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: To start with, it is a fact that throughout the world, women are likely to live many years longer than men, so they need money to support them during this time.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Since women are likely to be the ones left without a partner in old age, they may therefore have to pay for nursing care, because they don't have a spouse to look after them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Furthermore, the high divorce rates in North America are creating a poverty cycle for women.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It is the divorced women who will most often have to look after the children.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And thus, they need more money to look after not just themselves, but others.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So what can be done about this situation?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The population in North America is likely to contain an increasing number of elderly women.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The research indicates that at present for women, it takes a crisis to make them think about their future financial situation.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: But of course, this is the very worst time for anyone to make important decisions.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Women today need to look ahead, think ahead, not wait until they're under pressure.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Even women in their early 20s need to think about pensions.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: For example, and with increasing numbers of women in professional positions, there are signs that this is beginning to happen.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Then research also suggests that women avoid dealing effectively with their economic situation because of a lack of confidence.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The best way for them to overcome this is by getting themselves properly informed, so they are less dependent on other people's advice.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: A number of initiatives have been set up to help them do this.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This College, for example, is one of the educational institutions which offers night classes in Money Management, and increasing numbers of women are enrolling on such courses.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Here, they can be given advice on different ways of saving.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Many women are unwilling to invest in stocks and shares for instance, but these can be extremely profitable.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It is usually advised that at least 70% of a person's savings should be in low-risk investments, but for the rest, financial advisors often advise taking some well-informed risks.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Initiatives such as this can give women the economic skills and knowledge they need for a comfortable, independent retirement.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The increasing proportion of elderly women in the population is likely to have other economic consequences.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: But of course, this is the very worst time for anyone to make important decisions.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Women today need to look ahead, think ahead, not wait until they're under pressure.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Even women in their early 20s need to think about pensions.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: For example, and with increasing numbers of women in professional positions, there are signs that this is beginning to happen.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Then research also suggests that women avoid dealing effectively with their economic situation because of a lack of confidence.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The best way for them to overcome this is by getting themselves properly informed, so they are less dependent on other people's advice.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: A number of initiatives have been set up to help them do this.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: This College, for example, is one of the educational institutions which offers night classes in Money Management, and increasing numbers of women are enrolling on such courses.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Here, they can be given advice on different ways of saving.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Many women are unwilling to invest in stocks and shares for instance, but these can be extremely profitable.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: It is usually advised that at least 70% of a person's savings should be in low-risk investments, but for the rest, financial advisors often advise taking some well-informed risks.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Initiatives such as this can give women the economic skills and knowledge they need for a comfortable, independent retirement.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The increasing proportion of elderly women in the population is likely to have other economic consequences.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

However, women are more prepared to <input> about them.
Women tend to save for <input> and a house.
Men tend to save for <input> and for retirement.
Women who are left alone may have to pay for <input> when they are old.

desc.constraint: NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER

It is best for women to start thinking about pensions when they are in their <input> A good way for women to develop their <input> in dealing with financial affairs would be to attend classes in <input> When investing in stocks and shares, it is suggested that women should put a high proportion of their savings in <input> in such ways, women can have a comfortable, independent retirement.
body.title: Saving for the future
